title: draba oreades var. commutata
intro: Draba oreades var. commutata, a variant of the Alpine whitlow grass, is a flowering plant species characterized by its small white flowers and low-growing habit. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which are attributed to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Draba oreades var. commutata include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing its leaves in hot water to create a tea or by using its extract in ointments and salves to target specific health concerns.
